title: TSM Activation Gate Tripwire
description: Required artifact at runs/_tsm_activation.json. Read-and-validated by every real TSM runner (concept_validation.py / full_dataset_train.py / streaming_engine_export.py) before invoking the trainer or exporter. Codifies docs/planning/temporal_optimization_plan.md §0.2 activation gate as a runtime tripwire — converts the comment-only policy into a runtime check that survives stub-replacement.

schema_version.description: Schema version pin. Bump on field-shape changes OR closed-enum extension. Runners refuse files with unknown versions (exit 2). v1.0 (initial v1.2 commit) had a 3-tag enum; v1.1 (v1.3 commit) added far_distance_miss to make the enum a 4-tag set per plan §0.2 row 1. Activation files written against v1.0 schema must be regenerated.

selected_detector_artifact_sha256.description: SHA256 of the selected R2 detector's TRT engine .engine file (NOT best.pt — the engine is the deployment artifact whose backbone features TSM augments). Runner enforces THREE-WAY EQUALITY: activation_sha (this field) == sidecar.engine_sha256 (loaded from <selected_detector_artifact_path>.meta.json) == computed_sha (re-computed by runner against the file at selected_detector_artifact_path). Pairwise mismatch diagnostic: (a) activation != computed && sidecar == computed → 'stale activation tripwire — regenerate activation file'; (b) sidecar != computed && activation == computed → 'stale engine sidecar — regenerate sidecar via export script'; (c) activation == sidecar != computed → 'engine file changed without sidecar/activation update — rebuild engine'; (d) all three differ → 'full re-activation required'. Exit 2 with the matching diagnostic on any pair mismatch.

selected_detector_artifact_path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path to the selected detector TRT engine. Schema enforces: (1) does NOT start with '/' (absolute Unix paths rejected); (2) does NOT start with '<letter>:' (Windows drive prefixes rejected); (3) does NOT contain backslash (Windows-style separators rejected); (4) does NOT contain '..' as a path segment (parent-relative rejected); (5) ends with '.engine' (NOT .pt — engine is the deployment artifact). Runner asserts the file exists at this path AND its computed SHA256 equals selected_detector_artifact_sha256 AND matches the sidecar's engine_sha256 (see SHA truth-order in selected_detector_artifact_sha256.description).

replay_evidence_path.description: Repo-relative POSIX path to the on-vehicle replay analysis artifact (markdown report, JSON, or directory) that surfaced the approved failure modes. Schema enforces same exclusions as selected_detector_artifact_path (no leading '/', no '<letter>:', no backslash, no '..' segments) but no extension constraint. Runner additionally asserts the path exists (existence is filesystem-state, not schema-checkable).

approved_failure_mode_tags.description: Subset of failure modes documented in plan §0.2 启动判定 row 1 (小目标 / 远距离 / 遮挡 / 运动模糊导致漏检) that this TSM phase claims to address. Tag mapping: 小目标=small_target_miss, 远距离=far_distance_miss, 遮挡=occluded_miss, 运动模糊=motion_blur. far_distance and small_target are kept as separate tags because they are operationally distinct in this project's traffic-light setting (far-distance failures may come from low-resolution cues even when small-target metrics aren't tripped, and vice versa). Runner cross-checks the chosen subset against the phase's documented scope (concept_validation.py docstring); mismatch = exit 2. Enum is closed — adding a new tag requires schema_version bump.

activation_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this activation record was written (e.g. 2026-05-09T14:30:00Z).

activation_author.description: Optional. Person or system that approved the activation. Free-text.

notes.description: Optional. Free-text rationale for the chosen failure-mode tags. Audited at phase report time, not enforced.
